commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds SHA-256-backed session-file fence snapshots, accepts append-only runner-owned transcript tails during the released prompt window, exports `isAgentMessage`, and expands embedded session-lock regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main throws on any session-file stat mismatch after `releaseForPrompt`, and the PR supplies real gateway logs reproducing the false takeover on long tool-using channel turns.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can add the missing persisted-entry-base validation and focused regression without changing the broader session-lock design.

target.title: Discord: add reaction notification wake policy
target.body:
## Summary

- Add shared `notificationWake.reactions` policy support so notification-backed reaction events queue by default, can opt into immediate wake, or can be disabled with `off`.
- Wire Discord reaction notifications through that shared helper after existing Discord ingress authorization and routing.
- Preserve stable `reaction_key` values for standard/custom emoji and keep deduped reaction events from waking.
- Add heartbeat preflight handling so `notification-wake:*` wakes still run when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty, while generic notification events continue to honor the empty-file skip.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83164
- Related #47687
- Related #83611
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord reaction notifications now pass through a shared notification wake policy. Accepted reactions enqueue routed system events by default, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests a targeted `notification-wake:discord-reaction` heartbeat only after successful enqueue, `off` skips enqueue after ingress authorization, and deduped events do not wake.
- Real environment tested: redacted OpenClaw source checkout on PR head `31de50ea3b8e`. Earlier redacted Discord API canary proof on this PR verified the Discord bot/API reaction path and listener event shape without publishing secrets, usernames, chat titles, local paths, or raw IDs; the current head was reverified with focused policy/runtime regression tests and project checks below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
  - `pnpm config:channels:check`
  - `pnpm config:docs:check`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md docs/channels/discord.md extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/listeners.reactions.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.channels.ts src/config/types.discord.ts src/config/types.notifications.ts src/config/types.openclaw.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.channels-config.ts src/config/zod-schema.notifications.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts src/infra/notification-system-events.ts src/plugin-sdk/system-event-runtime.ts`
  - `git diff --check && git diff --cached --check`
  - `pnpm tsgo:prod`
  - `pnpm test:contracts:plugins`
- Evidence after fix:
  Redacted terminal output from the live Discord API canary for this PR's reaction listener path:

```json
{
  "discordBotIdentity": "verified_with_getCurrentUser",
  "canaryMessagePosted": true,
  "botReactionAddedViaRest": true,
  "guildHash": "01738befea7f",
  "channelHash": "5e33a98adfea",
  "messageHash": "9464114d77b7",
  "actorHash": "ab848dd6c34a",
  "systemEventQueued": true,
  "heartbeatRequestCallPresent": true,
  "sessionKeyHash": "7fbbcb1140b0",
  "eventTextShape": "Discord reaction added: <emoji> by <actor> on <guild> <channel> msg <message-id> (reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>) from <message-author>",
  "contextKeyHash": "d99c9ca6122b",
  "forceSenderIsOwnerFalse": true,
  "listenerLogs": []
}
```

  - Focused regression tests passed: 3 files, 92 tests.
  - Generated channel config check passed.
  - Generated docs baseline check passed with `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
  - Formatter reported all 21 touched files use the correct format.
  - Git whitespace checks exited cleanly.
  - Typecheck passed for core and extensions through `pnpm tsgo:prod`.
  - Plugin contracts passed: 62 files, 857 tests.
- Observed result after fix: default Discord reaction handling queues exactly one system event and does not request heartbeat; opt-in `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` requests `source: "notifications-event"`, `intent: "immediate"`, `reason: "notification-wake:discord-reaction"`, the routed `agentId`, and routed `sessionKey`; `off` skips enqueue; duplicate enqueue does not wake; custom emoji retain stable `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` context keys; `notification-wake:*` preflight bypasses empty `HEARTBEAT.md` skip while generic notification events do not.
- What was not tested: no production Discord Gateway restart was performed after this follow-up refactor. The prior redacted live Discord API canary on this PR covered external bot/API reaction reachability; this update adds exact current-head local runtime proof for the wake policy and heartbeat preflight behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the previous patch woke every accepted Discord reaction immediately, which made the behavior too aggressive and inconsistent with the shared notification policy in #83611.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Discord reaction notification enqueue was wired directly to heartbeat wake behavior instead of going through a configurable notification wake policy.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered reaction enqueue/routing but did not distinguish queue-by-default, opt-in wake, disabled enqueue, deduped no-wake, or empty-`HEARTBEAT.md` notification wake preflight.
- Contributing context (if known): Discord and Telegram reaction PRs were converging on similar semantics without a shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target tests or files:
  - `src/infra/notification-system-events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/discord/src/monitor.test.ts`
  - `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- Scenario the tests lock in: shared policy resolution precedence, queue default, explicit wake, explicit off, deduped no-wake, Discord listener wiring, custom emoji stable keys, and notification wake preflight through empty `HEARTBEAT.md`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests exercise the shared helper and Discord listener seam directly without broad channel startup or live gateway flake.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing Discord reaction tests covered authorization/routing and enqueue shape; this PR adds the missing wake-policy assertions.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Discord reaction notifications that pass existing access controls are queued on the routed session by default. Operators can set `channels.discord.notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` for immediate reaction-only turns or `"off"` to suppress reaction notification enqueueing after authorization. System event text keeps stable `reaction_key=emoji:<emoji>` and `reaction_key=custom_emoji:<id>` markers.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued]

Opt-in wake:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [routed system event queued] -> [notification-wake heartbeat]

Disabled:
[Discord reaction] -> [existing ingress authorization] -> [notificationWake.reactions=off] -> [skip enqueue]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: redacted local development environment
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord monitor harness and shared notification/heartbeat runtime tests
- Relevant config (redacted): default reaction policy, `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"`, and `notificationWake.reactions: "off"`

### Steps

1. Run the focused notification, Discord monitor, and heartbeat preflight tests.
2. Run generated config/docs checks.
3. Run formatter, whitespace, typecheck, and plugin contract checks.

### Expected

- Accepted reactions queue by default.
- Explicit wake policy requests the routed notification heartbeat after enqueue.
- Disabled policy skips enqueue.
- Deduped events do not wake.
- Empty `HEARTBEAT.md` does not suppress `notification-wake:*` turns.

### Actual

- Passed as described in the proof section.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: shared policy default/override resolution, Discord queue default, opt-in wake, off skip, deduped no-wake, custom emoji stable key, heartbeat preflight for notification wake, generated config/docs consistency, typecheck, plugin contract suite.
- Edge cases checked: blocked DM/guild/group reactions remain blocked by existing tests, reaction notification modes still gate behavior, generic notification events still skip when `HEARTBEAT.md` is empty.
- What you did **not** verify: production Discord Gateway delivery after this follow-up refactor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Optional new `notificationWake.reactions` config
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operators expecting every reaction to wake immediately need an explicit opt-in.
  - Mitigation: default queue preserves visibility without surprise model wakes; `notificationWake.reactions: "wake"` restores immediate processing intentionally.
- Risk: notification wake preflight could over-bypass empty `HEARTBEAT.md` gating.
  - Mitigation: the bypass is limited to `source: "notifications-event"` plus `reason` prefix `notification-wake:`; generic notification events still skip.

commands.author: kays0x

target.title: fix(agents): count user turns instead of stat for embedded session takeover fence
target.body:
## Summary

The embedded attempt session-lock fence uses a `stat()`-based fingerprint (`dev`, `ino`, `size`, `mtimeNs`, `ctimeNs`) to detect "another lane queued a new user turn against this session" while the runner releases its coarse lock for the LLM prompt window. The fingerprint is a proxy that overfires: every transcript-append the runner itself performs during the released window â€” `appendSessionTranscriptMessageLocked` writes tool calls, tool results, and assistant replies via `acquireSessionWriteLock(..., allowReentrant: true)` â€” changes the file's `size`/`mtimeNs`/`ctimeNs` without going through `refreshSessionFileFence`. Long DM turns with tool calls then trip `assertSessionFileFence` and throw `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` after the reply has already been delivered, surfacing a misleading "Agent failed before reply" message on top of a successful turn.

Keep the stat fingerprint as the fast path. On mismatch, only tolerate the runner's append-only assistant/tool transcript shape; treat everything else as a real takeover. The verification requires ALL of:

- **dev/ino match** â€” no atomic replacement onto a new inode.
- **birthtimeNs matches** â€” catches the unlink+recreate-same-inode case (common on tmpfs/ext4 with rapid recreation, where dev and ino are reused but the inode itself is fresh).
- **size grew** â€” file is append-only between fence-set and assert.
- **bytes `[0, fenceSize)` hash identical to the fenced prefix hash** â€” no in-place rewrite of earlier transcript content (catches compaction-style rewrites that happen to land on a same-byte boundary).
- **bytes `[fenceSize, currentSize)` are message entries with role `assistant` or `tool`** â€” no new user-role turn, no malformed tail.

Anything else â€” replacement, shrink, in-place rewrite, new user turn, or compaction â€” remains a real takeover. The fence snapshot captures the prefix SHA-256 alongside the fingerprint at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence` so the slow path can verify rigorously without keeping a file handle open.

Closes #83436.
Refs #83615 (this PR addresses the EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError surface; the issue also tracks unrelated kimi-k2.6 schema and DNS-fetch failures).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Embedded runner false-positive `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` fires after long Telegram DM turns whose reply was already delivered, surfacing a misleading "Agent failed before reply" message on top of a successful run.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw 2026.5.19-beta.1, Linux x86_64, Node 24.13.0. Long-running Telegram DM session against `claude-opus-4-7` (~3.5 min turn with multiple `exec` and `message` tool calls), reproduced reliably before this patch. Production behavior probed via runtime-patched `dist/selection-BpjGe-Y0.js` carrying the same fix semantics, then long DM runs (41sâ€“122s) exercised through the Telegram channel post-restart.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
CI=true pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
CI=true pnpm check:changed --staged=false
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Pre-patch failure mode, redacted runtime log from gateway (`/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-19.log`):
```
03:30:06.971  embedded run agent end: runId=bbd7d487 isError=false
03:30:07.008  embedded run prompt end: runId=bbd7d487 sessionId=<session-id> durationMs=217236
03:30:07.054  lane task error: lane=main durationMs=219867 error="EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /home/.../agents/main/sessions/<session-id>.jsonl"
03:30:07.071  Embedded agent failed before reply: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /home/.../<session-id>.jsonl
```
Note `isError=false` at `03:30:06.971` â€” the assistant turn and `message` tool delivery both succeeded â€” followed 47ms later by the takeover throw, surfacing as a misleading Telegram error on top of a delivered reply.

Post-patch behavior on the same gateway, four long DM runs after gateway restart at 03:46 EDT, every one delivering cleanly with zero takeover errors (redacted runtime log):
```
03:51:18  embedded run prompt end: runId=62395ffd  durationMs=122344  (no takeover)
03:52:39  embedded run prompt end: runId=67e21652  durationMs=41345   (no takeover)
04:05:05  embedded run prompt end: runId=a5994980  durationMs=101810  (no takeover)
04:07:34  embedded run prompt end: runId=a837440b  durationMs=94395   (no takeover)
```
The post-patch window contains only two "Embedded agent failed before reply" entries (at 04:05:06 and 04:07:35), both with cause `LLM request timed out` â€” unrelated to the takeover path. Zero `SessionTakeoverError` occurrences post-patch.

**Observed result after fix**: The renamed existing test (`rejects post-prompt writes when another owner queues a new user turn`) still asserts the takeover when a `role: "user"` entry is appended during the released window. Three new tests cover the runner's own assistant/tool appends (pass cleanly), in-place rewrite (rejected via prefix hash mismatch), and unlink+recreate-same-inode replacement (rejected via `birthtimeNs` mismatch even when the prefix bytes coincidentally match and the tail looks runner-owned). Targeted Vitest output:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  04:24:31
   Duration  884ms
```
`check:changed --staged=false` is clean: 0 lint warnings/errors across 8647 files, 0 import cycles, all typecheck/build/security/quality lanes pass.

**What was not tested**: Multi-process write contention on the same `sessionFile` from a different gateway process (no test infrastructure for cross-process locks in this surface). The prefix-hash check is `O(fenceSize)` I/O per fence-set; in production sessions seen so far (â‰¤ ~150 KB) this is sub-millisecond.

## Notes

- Adds three helpers near `readSessionFileFingerprint`: `FenceSnapshot` type, `snapshotSessionFileFence` (used at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence`), and `verifyAppendOnlyRunnerOwnedExtension` (used in the slow path of `assertSessionFileFence`). No new module-level dependencies beyond `node:crypto`'s `createHash`.
- Replaces one piece of controller state (`fenceFingerprint: SessionFileFingerprint | undefined`) with `fenceSnapshot: FenceSnapshot | undefined`. No new long-lived resources (no held file handles).
- This is the second revision of #84046; the first revision (count-only check) was flagged P1 by ClawSweeper for accepting same-user-count rewrites/replacements. This revision addresses that finding directly.
target.author: kays0x

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists

```
03:30:06.971  embedded run agent end: runId=bbd7d487 isError=false

```
Note `isError=false` at `03:30:06.971` â€” the assistant turn and `message` tool delivery both succeeded â€” followed 47ms later by the takeover throw, surfacing as a misleading Telegram error on top of a delivered reply.

Post-patch behavior on the same gateway, four long DM runs after gateway restart at 03:46 EDT, every one delivering cleanly with zero takeover errors (redacted runtime log):
```
03:51:18  embedded run prompt end: runId=62395ffd  durationMs=122344  (no takeover)
03:52:39  embedded run prompt end: runId=67e21652  durationMs=41345   (no takeover)
04:05:05  embedded run prompt end: runId=a5994980  durationMs=101810  (no takeover)
04:07:34  embedded run prompt end: runId=a837440b  durationMs=94395   (no takeover)
```
The post-patch window contains only two "Embedded agent failed before reply" entries (at 04:05:06 and 04:07:35), both with cause `LLM request timed out` â€” unrelated to the takeover path. Zero `SessionTakeoverError` occurrences post-patch.

**Observed result after fix**: The renamed existing test (`rejects post-prompt writes when another owner queues a new user turn`) still asserts the takeover when a `role: "user"` entry is appended during the released window. Three new tests cover the runner's own assistant/tool appends (pass cleanly), in-place rewrite (rejected via prefix hash mismatch), and unlink+recreate-same-inode replacement (rejected via `birthtimeNs` mismatch even when the prefix bytes coincidentally match and the tail looks runner-owned). Targeted Vitest output:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  04:24:31
   Duration  884ms
```
`check:changed --staged=false` is clean: 0 lint warnings/errors across 8647 files, 0 import cycles, all typecheck/build/security/quality lanes pass.

**What was not tested**: Multi-process write contention on the same `sessionFile` from a different gateway process (no test infrastructure for cross-process locks in this surface). The prefix-hash check is `O(fenceSize)` I/O per fence-set; in production sessions seen so far (â‰¤ ~150 KB) this is sub-millisecond.

## Notes

- Adds three helpers near `readSessionFileFingerprint`: `FenceSnapshot` type, `snapshotSessionFileFence` (used at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence`), and `verifyAppendOnlyRunnerOwnedExtension` (used in the slow path of `assertSessionFileFence`). No new module-level dependencies beyond `node:crypto`'s `createHash`.
- Replaces one piece of controller state (`fenceFingerprint: SessionFileFingerprint | undefined`) with `fenceSnapshot: FenceSnapshot | undefined`. No new long-lived resources (no held file handles).
- This is the second revision of #84046; the first revision (count-only check) was flagged P1 by ClawSweeper for accepting same-user-count rewrites/replacements. This revision addresses that finding directly.

commands.author: kays0x

target.title: fix(agents): count user turns instead of stat for embedded session takeover fence
target.body:
## Summary

The embedded attempt session-lock fence uses a `stat()`-based fingerprint (`dev`, `ino`, `size`, `mtimeNs`, `ctimeNs`) to detect "another lane queued a new user turn against this session" while the runner releases its coarse lock for the LLM prompt window. The fingerprint is a proxy that overfires: every transcript-append the runner itself performs during the released window â€” `appendSessionTranscriptMessageLocked` writes tool calls, tool results, and assistant replies via `acquireSessionWriteLock(..., allowReentrant: true)` â€” changes the file's `size`/`mtimeNs`/`ctimeNs` without going through `refreshSessionFileFence`. Long DM turns with tool calls then trip `assertSessionFileFence` and throw `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` after the reply has already been delivered, surfacing a misleading "Agent failed before reply" message on top of a successful turn.

Keep the stat fingerprint as the fast path. On mismatch, only tolerate the runner's append-only assistant/tool transcript shape; treat everything else as a real takeover. The verification requires ALL of:

- **dev/ino match** â€” no atomic replacement onto a new inode.
- **birthtimeNs matches** â€” catches the unlink+recreate-same-inode case (common on tmpfs/ext4 with rapid recreation, where dev and ino are reused but the inode itself is fresh).
- **size grew** â€” file is append-only between fence-set and assert.
- **bytes `[0, fenceSize)` hash identical to the fenced prefix hash** â€” no in-place rewrite of earlier transcript content (catches compaction-style rewrites that happen to land on a same-byte boundary).
- **bytes `[fenceSize, currentSize)` are message entries with role `assistant` or `tool`** â€” no new user-role turn, no malformed tail.

Anything else â€” replacement, shrink, in-place rewrite, new user turn, or compaction â€” remains a real takeover. The fence snapshot captures the prefix SHA-256 alongside the fingerprint at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence` so the slow path can verify rigorously without keeping a file handle open.

Closes #83436.
Refs #83615 (this PR addresses the EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError surface; the issue also tracks unrelated kimi-k2.6 schema and DNS-fetch failures).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Embedded runner false-positive `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` fires after long Telegram DM turns whose reply was already delivered, surfacing a misleading "Agent failed before reply" message on top of a successful run.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw 2026.5.19-beta.1, Linux x86_64, Node 24.13.0. Long-running Telegram DM session against `claude-opus-4-7` (~3.5 min turn with multiple `exec` and `message` tool calls), reproduced reliably before this patch. Production behavior probed via runtime-patched `dist/selection-BpjGe-Y0.js` carrying the same fix semantics, then long DM runs (41sâ€“122s) exercised through the Telegram channel post-restart.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
CI=true pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
CI=true pnpm check:changed --staged=false
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Pre-patch failure mode, redacted runtime log from gateway (`/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-19.log`):
```
03:30:06.971  embedded run agent end: runId=bbd7d487 isError=false
03:30:07.008  embedded run prompt end: runId=bbd7d487 sessionId=<session-id> durationMs=217236
03:30:07.054  lane task error: lane=main durationMs=219867 error="EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /home/.../agents/main/sessions/<session-id>.jsonl"
03:30:07.071  Embedded agent failed before reply: session file changed while embedded prompt lock was released: /home/.../<session-id>.jsonl
```
Note `isError=false` at `03:30:06.971` â€” the assistant turn and `message` tool delivery both succeeded â€” followed 47ms later by the takeover throw, surfacing as a misleading Telegram error on top of a delivered reply.

Post-patch behavior on the same gateway, four long DM runs after gateway restart at 03:46 EDT, every one delivering cleanly with zero takeover errors (redacted runtime log):
```
03:51:18  embedded run prompt end: runId=62395ffd  durationMs=122344  (no takeover)
03:52:39  embedded run prompt end: runId=67e21652  durationMs=41345   (no takeover)
04:05:05  embedded run prompt end: runId=a5994980  durationMs=101810  (no takeover)
04:07:34  embedded run prompt end: runId=a837440b  durationMs=94395   (no takeover)
```
The post-patch window contains only two "Embedded agent failed before reply" entries (at 04:05:06 and 04:07:35), both with cause `LLM request timed out` â€” unrelated to the takeover path. Zero `SessionTakeoverError` occurrences post-patch.

**Observed result after fix**: The renamed existing test (`rejects post-prompt writes when another owner queues a new user turn`) still asserts the takeover when a `role: "user"` entry is appended during the released window. Three new tests cover the runner's own assistant/tool appends (pass cleanly), in-place rewrite (rejected via prefix hash mismatch), and unlink+recreate-same-inode replacement (rejected via `birthtimeNs` mismatch even when the prefix bytes coincidentally match and the tail looks runner-owned). Targeted Vitest output:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  04:24:31
   Duration  884ms
```
`check:changed --staged=false` is clean: 0 lint warnings/errors across 8647 files, 0 import cycles, all typecheck/build/security/quality lanes pass.

**What was not tested**: Multi-process write contention on the same `sessionFile` from a different gateway process (no test infrastructure for cross-process locks in this surface). The prefix-hash check is `O(fenceSize)` I/O per fence-set; in production sessions seen so far (â‰¤ ~150 KB) this is sub-millisecond.

## Notes

- Adds three helpers near `readSessionFileFingerprint`: `FenceSnapshot` type, `snapshotSessionFileFence` (used at `releaseForPrompt` and `refreshSessionFileFence`), and `verifyAppendOnlyRunnerOwnedExtension` (used in the slow path of `assertSessionFileFence`). No new module-level dependencies beyond `node:crypto`'s `createHash`.
- Replaces one piece of controller state (`fenceFingerprint: SessionFileFingerprint | undefined`) with `fenceSnapshot: FenceSnapshot | undefined`. No new long-lived resources (no held file handles).
- This is the second revision of #84046; the first revision (count-only check) was flagged P1 by ClawSweeper for accepting same-user-count rewrites/replacements. This revision addresses that finding directly.
